game_name: World Class Track Meet / Stadium Events

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Tournament mode / Olympics mode available from start
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic running mechanics; player learns mat sensitivity and speed control via button row selection

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Tournament mode / Olympics mode available from start
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces timing-based obstacle avoidance; white box markers signal when to jump
Introduces run-up + sustained air time mechanics; single jump marker
Combines running with three separate timed jump markers; requires rhythmic coordination

typical_tension_triggers: Jump timing windows in long jump / triple jump
False start penalty (3 false starts = disqualification in tournament)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Event-based rhythm with discrete tension spikes per race. Tournament mode creates mounting pressure through sequential opponent matchups (6 opponents × 2 events each). Olympics mode distributes tension across 4 consecutive event types with difficulty escalation.

difficulty_curve.early_game: 100M dash establishes baseline mat control and speed ramping via button row selection
difficulty_curve.mid_game: 110M hurdles introduces reactive timing; long jump adds sustained input mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Triple jump combines all prior skills: running speed + three distinct timed jump windows; tournament progression faces increasingly difficult CPU opponents
notable_spikes: Tournament mode: final opponents (computer players 5-6) in both dash and hurdles
False start mechanic creates sudden failure states in tournament mode

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct event types arranged by complexity. Dash/hurdles use single-axis timing on left mat side. Jump events use either mat side with sustained input windows. Olympics mode chains all four events sequentially; tournament mode alternates dash and hurdles across 6 CPU opponents.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Olympics mode (all 4 events in sequence) vs Tournament mode (dash, then hurdles, repeating)

notable_level_patterns.description: Tournament mode creates mounting difficulty by stacking six sequential computer opponents. Each opponent must be defeated at both dash and hurdles before progressing. Three false starts result in instant disqualification.

Hurdles use reactive white box markers. Long jump uses single jump marker at distance. Triple jump uses three separate jump markers. Difficulty increases with marker frequency and precision requirements.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 110M hurdles (variable marker frequency) vs triple jump (three markers in rapid succession)

sources_used: tcrf - Family Fun Fitness: Stadium Events (gameplay mechanics, events list, tournament mode details)
wikipedia - Stadium Events (gameplay section with event descriptions, tournament and Olympics modes, false start mechanics)
